task_completion.description: Navigate to the OpenMRS Administration interface
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the OpenMRS Administration dashboard, typically featuring a classic green and white UI with various management links.

task_completion.description: Access the Person Attribute Type management page
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the 'Person Attribute Type' creation form or the list of existing Person Attribute Types.
The text 'Preferred Language' is visible in the 'Name' input field on the Person Attribute Type form.
The text 'java.lang.String' is visible in the 'Format' input field on the Person Attribute Type form.
The text 'Patient's preferred language for translation and interpreter services' is visible in the 'Description' textarea.
The checkbox next to 'Searchable' is checked on the Person Attribute Type form.

task_completion.description: Save the new Person Attribute Type
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success message (e.g., 'Person Attribute Type saved') is visible, or 'Preferred Language' appears in the list of saved Person Attribute Types.

integrity.description: The agent used a web browser to interact with the OpenMRS interface
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser (like Chrome or Firefox) being used to navigate the UI, rather than terminal commands like curl.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual OpenMRS application
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show the genuine OpenMRS Administration interface, not a text editor, mock-up, or local HTML file.

integrity.description: The agent did not use scripts to bypass the UI
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of the agent writing Python, bash, or other scripts in a terminal or IDE to create the attribute type via API.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target Person Attribute Type must be named 'Preferred Language' with the format 'java.lang.String'. The description must contain the keyword 'language' (specifically 'Patient\'s preferred language for translation and interpreter services'). The 'Searchable' option must be enabled (checked). The OpenMRS administration interface is located at https://localhost/openmrs/admin.
